school.name: The Schoolhouse
school.description: Each teacher at the schoolhouse leads a different class in a different room. Work out who teaches what, and where.
clues: Boyd's period is higher than both the member of the Robotics club's period and the class with 76%'s period.
If either Period 1 goes with Robotics or Period 5 goes with Library, then Frost does not go with Studio.
Frost goes with none of Studio, Yearbook, or 74%.
Either Yearbook goes with Boyd or Chess goes with Period 3.
72% goes with either Ames or Pavilion (but not both).
Cloister, 72%, and 78% go with Choir, Rowing, and Period 1, in some order.

Period 4 goes with exactly three of Carver, Cloister, Chess, and 74%.
Yearbook goes with none of Ames, Carver, or 72%.

If 72% does not go with Period 1, then all of Chess goes with 76%, Ames goes with Period 2, and Pavilion goes with Robotics.

chess.name: The Chess Club
chess.description: Regulars at the chess club each swear by a different opening, a favourite tactic, and a prized set. Work out who plays what.
clues: Magnus' placing is higher than both the player who loves the Pin's placing and the 1893-rated player's placing.
If either 5th goes with Pin or 1st goes with French, then Vera does not go with Pirc.
Vera goes with none of Pirc, Zugzwang, or 1843.
Either Zugzwang goes with Magnus or Fork goes with 3rd.
1793 goes with either Boris or London (but not both).
Benoni, 1793, and 1943 go with Gambit, Skewer, and 5th, in some order.
Exactly one of these is true: French goes with 1843; Sicilian goes with 2nd; 1893 goes with 2nd.

The French player's placing is between the 1893-rated player's placing and the player who loves the Skewer's placing.
If 1793 does not go with 5th, then all of Fork goes with 1893, Boris goes with 4th, and London goes with Pin.
